  • Strategic Planning Scenarios in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Universal Model

Finance & Risk

  • AI assisted creation of balance validation rules AND comment proposal
  • Financial Reporting according to IFRS18 in S4HANA Cloud
  • Financial Statements in the New Review Booklets Analytical Application

Public Cloud

  • Actual Liquidity Analysis
  • AI Agent for Open Item Clearing
  • AI-powered chatbot (Joule) for Intelligent Margin Analysis
  • Enhancing Payment Run Experience
  • Price Recommendation in S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition
  • Research: Payment Advice In Existing Business Process

Details on all 53 Projects can be found here or in the following Blog. 

Read Customer Influence and Adoption to learn more about our customer engagement initiatives and customer influence programs. 

Let's shape the future of our Finance solutions together!

We look forward to hearing from you. 

Best regards,

Helen

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for group reporting - Release 2502.4 is generally available
entry.content:
We're happy to announce the release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud for group reporting 2502.4, delivered on June 23, 2025.

Here's what's new:

  • Conversational search with Joule – our AI copilot now lets you ask questions in natural language and get clear, summarized answers from SAP Help Portal content.
  • A refreshed Post Group Journal Entries app – now with an accountant-friendly layout and intuitive debit/credit indicators.
  • A redesigned consolidation monitor – enjoy one-click actions and advanced scheduling to save time and reduce complexity.
  • Our first release of the new consolidation rule engine – offering flexible automation for unit-dependent postings.

These improvements are built to simplify your financial closing, improve user satisfaction, and accelerate your group reporting process.

Download our feature deck.

--
Conversational search through Joule, our AI copilot

Joule is the AI copilot that truly understands your business. Joule revolutionizes how you interact with your SAP business systems, making every touch-point count and every task simpler. It enables the companion of the Intelligent Enterprise, guiding you through content discovery within the SAP Ecosystem, and giving a transparent role-based access to the relevant processes from everywhere. This is the one assistant experience, a unified and delightful user experience across SAP’s solution portfolio.

Joule offers conversational interactions between humans and computers to simplify access to information and automate business processes, thus improving both the employee and customer satisfaction. Joule can direct you to the right application to complete your tasks, help you to complete certain tasks, and find the information you're looking for quickly and easily.

Available now, Joule’s informational capability (Conversational search) in Group Reporting helps you retrieve necessary information from the SAP Help Portal or documents uploaded to the Joule service if you've Set Up Document Grounding. 

Joule searches available documentation and its own knowledge base to find the answer to your query, summarizes the search results and provides the summarization as the answer to your question, along with the top three search results. The screenshots below show conversation examples around consolidation simulations, in multiple languages.

For more information: 

  • What is Joule? | SAP Help Portal
  • Joule Capabilities | SAP Help Portal 
  • Conversational search through Joule, our AI copilot | Group Reporting 2502.4 feature deck

--
One-Click Consolidation in a New Consolidation Monitor

The consolidation monitor gives you a graphic overview of consolidation units and consolidation groups, along with tasks and task groups. You can use it as a central work environment where you're able to execute tasks for collecting, standardizing, and consolidating all reported financial data. At the same time, you can monitor the progress of these tasks for each individual consolidation unit or group.

Available now, we deliver a new version of the Consolidation Monitor. You can now work in a modern user interface powered by the latest Fiori technology, including features like filtering and sorting. To enhance your closing process experience, you're able to perform several actions with just one click – for example, you can carry out a full consolidation or open and close periods effortlessly. You can improve usability by switching from the existing app to this new version with its updated interface. You're able to increase efficiency by performing actions across multiple values, including tasks, groups, and periods. Additionally, you can streamline operations by opening or closing multiple periods, running full consolidations, and automating task processing through task scheduling.

With the new Consolidation Monitor, you're able to:

  • Filter by versions, multiple consolidation groups, periods, tasks, or statuses to quickly find what you need
  • View a list of tasks with detailed status information, such as the last execution time, who last changed it, the number of warnings or errors, the period status, and the data monitor status
  • Run a full consolidation, executing all automatic tasks
  • Process multiple consolidation groups
  • Process multiple fiscal periods by bundling them in a process period
  • Open/close periods for multiple consolidation groups and fiscal periods 
  • Define processing periods that allow you to handle multiple data-carrying periods within a single processing period periods) at once
  • Schedule a full consolidation in “Schedule Jobs for Consolidation Tasks”
  • Schedule remaining tasks, including custom tasks, to streamline your workflow
  • Schedule the entire monitor to automate and optimize further your consolidation process.
  • Perform multitask actions like running, blocking, and unblocking tasks
  • Carry out multiple period actions, including opening or closing periods

To run a full consolidation, simply select “Run Full Consolidation” (screenshot below). The parameters are automatically carried over from the filters, and all predefined consolidation tasks will be executed. If the process involves multiple consolidation groups, they will be handled in alphabetical order. 

You can also run tasks or update their status individually by selecting the task, clicking “Actions,” and choosing the appropriate option. In addition, you can select and run multiple automatic tasks together (screenshot below). To run a manual posting task, select a single manual task and click “Manual Run.”

You can still use the "old" Consolidation Monitor as a fallback. However, we recommend getting familiar with the new Consolidation Monitor in a test environment first, and then fully transitioning to it at the beginning of a new process period.

For more information: 

  • Consolidation Monitor (New Version) | SAP Help Portal 
  • New Consolidation Monitor | Group Reporting 2502.4 Feature Deck

--
New Consolidation Rule Engine - Increased Flexibility in Automating Consolidation Unit Dependent Postings

As part of our roadmap acceleration program, we’re developing a new consolidation rule engine in Group Reporting. This engine is inspired by SAP Financial Consolidation and aims to offer the same level of power and flexibility valued by its users. The delivery will occur in three phases. With this release (2502.4) we’re introducing the automation of consolidation unit dependent postings. Moving forward in 2025, our plans include the automation of inter-company postings and the automation of consolidation group-dependent postings.

With release 2502.4, you can now define flexible rules to automate consolidation unit dependent postings on non-consolidated data (posting levels 00, 0C, 01, 10). These rules can replace the existing tasks of annual net income and reclassifications. For example, you can calculate the net income based on the reported data and specify which additional or custom fields should be retained. You can also enrich the reported data by factoring in the G/L account and assigning a specific partner unit. Additionally, for selected consolidation units, you can reclassify Inventory of Finished Goods as Inventory of Semi-Finished Goods.

Our new rule definition framework provides you with improved usability, increased flexibility and robustness. You’re now able to:

  • Define source data flexibly
  • Assign multiple document types in a single task
  • Create multiple posting lines
  • Define target data flexibly, including inheritance from selected data and value calculation
  • Assign document types at the individual rule level
  • Include results from previous rule steps as an option
  • Assign rules to rule groups
  • Assign rule groups to tasks
  • Utilize a modern task log

The key apps you can use to manage automatic postings are:

(1) Manage Rules for Automatic Postings - Use this app to create, change, copy and display posting rules and rule groups and activate them for the automatic posting of group journal entries.

(2) Assign Rule Groups for Automatic Postings - Use this app to assign rule groups to consolidation tasks. The consolidation task then carries out the rules included in the assigned rule group. 

(3) Import/Export Current Settings - Use this app to export your specified  posting rules or posting rule groups to a spreadsheet, or import any rules or rule groups exported from another system.

(4) Task Logs - Use this app to check the logs of each consolidation task along the consolidation process.

When you open the "Manage Rules for Automatic Postings" app, you can search and filter rule groups and rules using various criteria, including a search bar, editing status (such as All, Drafts, or Locked), status (Active or Inactive), chart of accounts, and the user who created or last changed the rule.

Managing rules

To create a rule, just click on "Create". As shown in the screenshot below, in a rule, you define the source and target data, and the posting lines.

(1) Source Data – You can define how to select the source data using a selection object, a period selection, and the fields to read. You're able to restrict the source data by selecting a range of master data, defining a period interval, and choosing specific fields to read, which improves rule performance and log readability.

(2) Target Data – You can choose to post on the original document type or a single document type that you select. You're able to select which measures must be posted by the rule, overriding the document type selection. This ensures accurate posting of values in the target data.

(3) Posting Lines – The overview summarizes the posting line definition, and clicking on a posting line displays its details. You can define the field values and calculate the amounts to post. You're able to edit details like field assignments and value calculations for each posting line. 

Managing rule groups

You can define Rule Groups to select and organize the rules that need to be executed when running an automatic postings task in the Data Monitor. For example, you're able to use the Rule Group NI_RCL to execute three rules: calculating net income (for both released and reported data), and reclassifying inventory of finished goods. You can assign the Rule Group to the task using the "Assign Rule Groups for Automatic Postings" app.

From the overview page, open your rule group (1). The rule group page displays nodes (2) and rules (3). When clicking a rule, you can navigate to its definition. You can define that one rule depends on previous rules. This is needed when the source data of the current rule includes data posted by the previous rule.

Import / export settings

Additionally, you can export the rule groups, rules, rule group assignments, and selections from one system to import them in a different system. This is particularly useful when you want to export the validation or posting settings that are defined, activated, and tested in the system you use for testing and import them to your production system.

For more information:

  • Automation of consolidation unit dependent postings | SAP Help Portal
  • Automation of consolidation unit dependent postings | Group Reporting 2502 feature deck
  • Group Reporting Roadmap Acceleration Program | SAP Blog
  • Consolidation auto-post rules engine (new rule engine)  | SAP Roadmap Explorer 

--
Extending Usability in Creating Manual Journals Using a Debit/Credit Layout

With release 2502.4, we enhance the usability of the "Post Group Journal Entries" app. You can choose to enter manual group journals with a Debit/Credit indicator and sign-free amounts. When you activate this new layout, the manual journal entry is more accountant-oriented. This new feature changes only the display of the journals; it doesn’t change the way they're stored in the database. It's also applied to previously posted journals.

(1) When you activate the Debit/Credit indicator in the Post Group Journal Entries app, you can now see a new column called “Debit/Credit”, which lets you choose between two values: “Debit” or “Credit.” 

(2) You're able to enter the amounts in local, group, or transaction currency, as well as the quantity, without needing to use a sign. 

(3) A new “Balance” section lets you view the balance by field type, currency/unit, and version (for extension versions). For local and group currency, you’ll see a green highlight when the entry is balanced (total is zero), and a red highlight when it’s not. The amounts in transaction currency and quantity don’t need to be balanced, so they aren’t color-coded.

For more information: 

  • Debit/Credit Indicator in the Manual Group Journal Entries Apps | SAP Help Portal 
  • Debit/Credit Indicator | Group Reporting 2502 feature deck

--
More information

  • Group Reporting 2502.4 Feature Deck | sap.com
  • Group Reporting | SAP Help Portal
  • Group Reporting new features recap | SAP Community
  • Group Reporting community | SAP Community
  • Group Reporting customer influence platform | SAP Customer Influence

entry.title: Help influence the next AI Features for My Home on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
My Home is the starting point for SAP S/4HANA. It contains all your to-do items, news feeds, apps, and insights tiles and cards. Artificial intelligence is available throughout the page, including alerts to your to-dos, recommended apps, based on your role, and the ability to create new cards using natural language. Of course, Joule is also available to help you find the information you need to run your business.

We are continuing to enhance the capabilities in My Home and would love to hear your feedback on two powerful enhancements designed to improve usability and streamline everyday tasks in SAP applications. To collect your ideas, we are running two new customer engagement initiatives. These are open to customers and partners running SAP Cloud ERP (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)

This initiative focuses on:

  • AI-assisted Smart Visualization in My Home – Situations, which provides resolution suggestions directly on the homepage for faster task completion (https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3931)
  • AI-powered error resolution, including natural language input and intelligent suggestions to help users fix system errors quickly and intuitively (https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3929)

These innovations aim to reduce training time, boost productivity, and make the SAP experience more seamless and intelligent.

SAP Products in Focus

  • My Home page
  • SAP Fiori apps using the SAP Fiori elements framework

Why Join?

  • Get early access to upcoming innovations
  • Help shape tools that better fit your day-to-day needs
  • Collaborate with SAP designers, developers, and product experts
  • Influence real product enhancements with your feedback

 Your input now will help improving the user experience of SAP Cloud ERP, helping other customers save time and simplify business processes.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2504 FD2
entry.content:
With the latest feature delivery (2504 FD2) for SAP Central Business Configuration, exciting new features are available. This blog post will provide you with a comprehensive overview.

Enhanced Transparency for Team Management

We’re pleased to announce a recent improvement to the Settings page. In an effort to increase transparency, we’ve introduced a new column to the Team table. This column will now display the e-mail addresses of the users assigned to the workspace, making it easier for you to manage and oversee your team members.

E-Mail column in the Team table

Optimized User Experience for Switching On Demo Data

We’ve enhanced the user experience for assigning deployment targets, giving the Assign Deployment Target dialog box a fresh new look. Depending on the system type, you may now have the option to easily switch demo data on or off up until scope confirmation. In addition, this action is logged as a dedicated entry in the Change Logs app to support audit requirements.

Demo Data column in the Assign Deployment Target dialog box

For a quick summary, check out this video about What's New in 2504 & FD2. Don’t forget to also use the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal, which provides further details and links about all the recent changes.

That wraps up the new features this time. Did you like this blog? If so, please leave us a thumbs up at the top. Also, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have any ideas on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the Customer Influence platform to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Q2 ‘25 Release Highlights
entry.content:
In the "2025 State of B2B eCommerce Report," written in collaboration with MasterB2B, many organizations expressed challenges with transitioning from analog-focused operations to digital-centric models. The difficulty is compounded by a lack of digital expertise and specialization among many companies. 

 We understand these challenges, and in Q2 2025 we introduced numerous B2B commerce improvements, including the announcement of the B2B Self-Service Portal at SAP Sapphire, designed to support organizations in easing into this digital transformation and meeting growing B2B customer expectations. 

We invite you to explore these B2B enhancements and other important SAP Commerce Cloud updates from the last three months by joining our Q2 2025 Release Highlights webcast on July 8 to stay updated on our latest advancements. A replay will be available afterwards. 

 

Adaptive 


Accelerate the growth of your B2B commerce business quickly 
 SAP B2B Self-Service Portal is an SAP Cloud ERP add-on designed to provide customers with post-purchase transparency without the time, effort, or expense involved in a comprehensive commerce implementation. Customers can start small and launch in a matter of weeks using partner implementation packages. You can later expand to SAP Commerce Cloud, all without requiring data or environment migration. 

Buyers benefit by receiving real-time access to order information, delivery status, and invoices, reducing the load on inside sales and support teams. This allows your teams to focus on building relationships and value-generating tasks, leading to higher customer satisfaction and lower overall service costs. 

B2B Self-Service Portal 

Enhance the purchasing experience for B2B buyers 
 

SAP Commerce Cloud, Open Payment Framework launched in phases starting in Q4 2024, initially focusing on B2C payment processes. We have now expanded to include B2B payment capabilities, offering your B2B customers a streamlined and secure payment experience. This framework ensures proven security with built-in validation features, error handling, and feature toggles for a seamless, backward-compatible rollout. 

Additionally, to enhance customer flexibility, we support both account and digital payment options, all managed centrally through the Open Payment Framework. 

Improved useability and security with new SmartEdit enhancements 
 
We have made many improvements to SmartEdit to give users more control, flexibility, and security. 
Enhanced Page View gives you the ability to customize your page view based on your preferences. You can now show and hide columns, reorder columns, sort columns, change column width, and define the number of items to be displayed per page. The customization options enable more control and a smoother workflow. 

The Preview Selector has been enhanced to support the preview of content catalog versions for specific user groups, giving those groups access to see precisely the content you intend for them to view.  
Please view this article for more details on these SmartEdit updates.

 

Insightful 

 

Unlock the power of AI-driven commerce 

Elevate shopping experiences with our enhanced AI-based Shopping Agent. The Shopping Agent now lets customers add products to their cart, as well as provide the order number, date, shipping address and total, with conversational updates.  
 
The conversation flows naturally and saves the customer time with fast responses and actions. 

Add item to cart with Shopping Agent 

Streamline product management with reusable prompt templates for generating AI-powered product descriptions. Admins can create and manage prompt templates to save time while ensuring consistency and tailored branding across the product catalog. 

You can now easily connect AI Capabilities for SAP Commerce Cloud through Joule Premium for CX with external Product Information Management (PIM) systems and configure via Omni Commerce Connect Module (OCC). This offers unmatched flexibility and streamlines operations with the added AI capabilities.  

 

Connected 

 

 Automate notifications and ensure its delivery 

Use our configurable Event Notifications Framework to easily integrate with the system of your choice. We offer over 140 ready-to-use events like Order Placed, User Account Deleted, Product Reviewed, and many other transactional, user-based or backend events.  

Our Framework lets you send real-time messages to third-party systems by setting up Webhooks in Backoffice with a visual configurator. It simplifies integrations by reducing the need for custom code while increasing versatility. 

 To guarantee notifications make it to their destination, we’ve now introduced At-least-once delivery (ALOD) that automatically resends outbound webhook events that encounter failures. This ensures that crucial events are successfully delivered at least one time, safeguarding critical information. 

Webhook Events with At Least Once Delivery enabled 

Offer purchase flexibility with B2B PunchOut for Composable Storefront 

B2B Punchout makes it easy for buyers to explore a supplier's online catalog and conveniently save chosen items as a requisition in their procurement system. Users are automatically logged in and out through a seamless login process. 
 
With B2B PunchOut on the Composable Storefront, the shopping cart of your headless storefront serves as the basis for creating a purchase requisition, which is then sent for approval within the buyer's company. Once approved, the order is sent back to the supplier's site for processing. There's no checkout button on the cart page. Instead, when you're ready to complete your purchase, simply select "Back to Requisition" to wrap up the transaction in your procurement system. 
 

Shopping cart page in PunchOut session 
 

Display relevant documents on sales orders 

In the latest S/4HANA Order Management integration, you can now display documents in the order history, saving time and providing relevant product information. Documents attached to sales orders in SAP S/4HANA Sales, such as product specifications or special instructions, are displayed to provide greater visibility and convenience. 

Gain scalable B2B pricing with SAP Variant Configuration Pricing integration  

B2B customers gain scalable access to SAP S/4HANA pricing features for standard and configurable products during their shopping journey. 

This can be used as a pricing engine. The SAP Commerce Cloud integration with SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing enables complex B2B pricing use cases for physical and configurable products in product list and detail pages as well cart calculation and checkout.

Elevate your after-sales experience with Reverse.io 

We are excited to partner with Revers.io and expand SAP Order Management Services returns to offer a pre-composed solution that improves the post-sales experience. Revers.io streamlines return, repair, and product reuse processes. 

Benefit from an integrated self-service return and repair process, which connects automated workflows across the after-sales chain, reducing lead times and cutting reverse logistics costs. Additionally, the solution accelerates time-to-market and lessens the customer service workload. 

 

Revers.io 
 

Boost store associate productivity with Newstore 

Newstore has introduced a mobile-first, SAP-ready solution with pre-built integrations across SAP commerce solutions to deliver faster, smarter service in retail stores. The user-friendly associate app simplifies workflows, minimizes reliance on hardware, and bridges the gap between digital and in-store retail. Newstore enables efficient store fulfillment, task management, and personalized service to improve operations in high-touch retail environments. 

NewStore 

 

Additional 

 

Prepare for the busiest shopping season with 2025 Holiday Readiness Program 

The 2025 Holiday Season Readiness Program is designed to help you prepare for peak shopping periods. Register by August 29 to ensure smooth operations.  

The program is open to all SAP Commerce Cloud customers planning promotions for major holiday events like Singles’ Day, El Buen Fin, Black Friday/Cyber Monday, and the Christmas season. Though primarily aimed at B2C businesses, B2B and B2B2C customers expecting higher traffic during these times are also encouraged to participate. 

For more details on the above release features, check out the following resources: 

  • SAP Commerce Cloud Release Ready Kit 
  • Performance Optimization Best Practices 
  • SAP Commerce Cloud update cycle 
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Release Navigator single access point to release content 

SAP Help Portal 

  • SAP Commerce Cloud What’s New 

Please share your feedback in a comment and follow our SAP Commerce Cloud topic page to connect with the community!
